articles.title: Is AI Going to Take PM Jobs?
articles.summary: AI will reshape product management, not eliminate it. A broader technology downturn poses a greater threat to PM jobs than automation.

articles.title: The Prototype Is a Question, Not a Product
articles.summary: AI makes convincing prototypes easier to build, but polish can create a false sense of product readiness. A clear decision framework helps teams define what each prototype can prove, gather the right evidence, and prevent experimental code from becoming an accidental production commitment.

articles.title: Are PMs becoming irrelevant?
articles.summary: AI is replacing routine PM work, not strategic product judgment. The PMs who thrive will focus on choosing the right problems and creating measurable value.

articles.title: The Farabi's Guide to Building Products That Matter
articles.summary: Great products help users flourish rather than merely capture attention. Shared purpose, careful research, and continuous iteration keep teams focused on that goal.

articles.title: How to Create a Truly Inspiring Product Vision
articles.summary: A strong product vision captures the lasting positive change a product aims to create, rather than describing features or business goals. Concise, emotionally engaging, and collaboratively developed, it motivates teams and provides stable direction for product strategy.

articles.title: All Things Product Podcast with Teresa Torres & Petra Wille (17 minute video)
articles.summary: Customer data can reveal what to investigate, but rarely what to build. Rich, story-based interviews provide the context teams need to make better product decisions.

articles.title: Reactors Have a Surprising Amount of Detail
articles.summary: Early momentum can hide the details that determine whether a product succeeds. Rigorous testing and continuous iteration turn promising technology into something reliable.

articles.title: Code Yellow, Code Red
articles.summary: Code Yellows help teams resolve serious engineering problems before they become emergencies. Clear ownership, measurable exit criteria, and transparent communication keep the response focused and temporary.

articles.title: AI products generate more signal than teams know how to use
articles.summary: AI conversations reveal what users want and where products fall short.

articles.title: Generated and suppressed demand
articles.summary: Improving teams often uncover hidden demand, causing their backlogs to grow again.
